_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-39R Pralidoxime (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-39R: Pralidoxime — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
pharmacological_category: Antidote — Acetylcholinesterase Reactivator (Organophosphate / Nerve Agent Antidote)
precautions: Used in conjunction with atropine (9-15R) — pralidoxime reactivates acetylcholinesterase; atropine blocks muscarinic receptors
Administer as soon as possible after nerve agent/organophosphate exposure — efficacy decreases with time as acetylcholinesterase ages
600 mg per nerve agent auto-injector kit — use same chart (Chart A and Chart B) as atropine
When nerve agent kit is unavailable: pralidoxime 1-2 grams IV over 15-30 minutes may be used when available; contact online MC
expected_effects: Reactivation of acetylcholinesterase enzyme inhibited by organophosphate compounds
Reversal of nicotinic effects (muscle weakness, paralysis) — complements atropine's muscarinic reversal
Improvement in muscle strength and reduced nicotinic toxidrome
notes: Michigan nerve agent kit: 600 mg pralidoxime per auto-injector; administer per Chart A and Chart B with atropine
Pralidoxime must accompany atropine when nerve agent kit is available
SLUDGE/SLUDGEM symptoms (Salivation, Lacrimation, Urination, Defecation, GI distress, Emesis, Miosis) indicate muscarinic toxicity — treat with atropine
Nicotinic effects (muscle weakness, fasciculations, flaccid paralysis) — pralidoxime primary target

dosing.population: All — nerve agent exposure / organophosphate poisoning
dosing.dose: 600 mg per nerve agent auto-injector kit per Chart A and Chart B (same as atropine protocol)
dosing.notes: Administer with atropine concurrently; 600 mg per auto-injector; repeat per nerve agent protocol

used_in_protocols: Toxicological Emergencies — Nerve Agent / Organophosphate Poisoning
